recent_trades.summary: Increasing MA to 5% on the analyst upgrade viewing the dip below $500 as a gift for long-term investors plus positive sustainability/tech trends, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: MA has -1.61% unrealized P/L but +0.15 sentiment, a specific rating upgrade, and benefits from digital payments growth linked to broader AI/economic expansion. Sizing lifts it from 3% toward peer levels like other financials while staying well under 20% single-name cap; funded by the GOOGL trim.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming GOOGL to 3% of portfolio to free capital from a laggard with neutral sentiment and -1.18% unrealized loss while AI peers advance.
recent_trades.reasoning: GOOGL shows flat-to-negative momentum versus the +1-4% moves in NVDA/ORCL/AVGO; news flow is neutral with no clear catalyst compared to stronger AI sentiment elsewhere. Partial trim (not full exit) maintains some exposure while funding higher-conviction reallocation.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Chevron from 2.92% to 2% on negative sentiment from weak statutory earnings report, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: CVX sentiment turned negative with a SeekingAlpha article highlighting weak statutory earnings. Despite strong energy sector tailwinds, the recent buy yesterday may be premature. Reducing the position to 2% limits downside while maintaining energy exposure.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming T-Mobile from 4.79% to 3.5% due to negative sentiment from the rural dead zone joint venture news, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: TMUS sentiment turned negative with headlines about a joint venture to address rural dead zones, which may pressure margins. The stock is down slightly from my cost. Reducing the overweight position frees capital for reallocation to higher-conviction names like MA.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to QCOM as the stock is soaring today on China trade pivot news with positive sentiment +0.28 and multiple analyst calls positioning it well.
recent_trades.reasoning: QCOM is specifically featured in 'Why Is Qualcomm Stock Soaring Today' and '5 Companies Betting on Trump's China Pivot' — a meaningful new catalyst since my last trim. The China FSD/trade thaw is a direct tailwind for QCOM's Snapdragon business. Currently underwater at -5.61% but the China catalyst is fresh. Raising from ~3% to 5% weight to recapture some of this move.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating SBUX on TD Cowen upgrade to Buy citing expected margin recovery, with positive sentiment +0.31 and analyst tailwinds.
recent_trades.reasoning: TD Cowen upgraded SBUX to Buy today specifically citing expected margin recovery, which is a fundamental catalyst not yet fully priced in. Sentiment is +0.31, stock is near 30-day lows (~107.66 vs 108.88 high), offering a reasonable entry point. Wall Street research coverage is positive. Small initial position at 4% to test the thesis with room to add.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating small SBUX position on TD Cowen upgrade to Buy citing margin recovery, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Fresh analyst upgrade is a concrete catalyst. Stock is near 30-day lows which provides good entry. Sentiment is +0.31. Starting small at 3% given it's a new position in a defensive name.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Qualcomm slightly again as it remains a weaker position despite better headlines, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: QCOM does have improving sentiment and a pop headline, but it is still one of the few holdings sitting at a notable unrealized loss and remains below its 30-day high. This is only a modest reduction because positive news could continue to help, but I want less capital tied up in a lagging setup.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting the remaining Mastercard stub because it is too small to matter and has lagged our stronger holdings, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: We already trimmed MA recently and the residual 0.5% weight adds complexity without meaningful contribution. While the news tone is mildly positive, the stock remains below prior levels and the capital is better recycled into names showing stronger current catalysts.

recent_trades.summary: Starting Starbucks on a fresh analyst upgrade and improving recovery narrative, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: SBUX has one of the stronger positive sentiment readings in this run and the TD Cowen upgrade adds a same-day catalyst that is more actionable than generic market commentary. The chart is stable after recent weakness and a small starter position fits the still-early confirmation while preserving flexibility for later intraday runs.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting NEM fully as the position is underwater (-1.95%) and today's headline questions whether the 148% rally is already priced in.

Despite positive sentiment (+0.29), the stock is near its 30-day low. Capital is better deployed in AI momentum names with clearer near-term catalysts. Exiting frees ~1.7% for reallocation.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming QCOM from 5.7% to 3% to free capital for higher-conviction AI plays; position remains underwater at -5.1% despite positive news today.
recent_trades.reasoning: QCOM was initiated yesterday on China trade optimism but is still -5.1% underwater with avg cost of $215.81 vs current $204.74. While sentiment today is positive (+0.28) and there's China pivot news, I'm reallocating capital to AMD, AVGO, and TSLA which have stronger immediate catalysts and cleaner chart setups. Keeping a small 3% position to benefit if the China trade theme continues.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating 5% ORCL position on strong AI infrastructure momentum and positive analyst coverage, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: ORCL leads universe sentiment at +0.36 with headlines confirming AI bet gaining support and questions on whether it remains undervalued after gains; no current holding allows clean 5% entry. This diversifies AI exposure beyond existing NVDA/AVGO while technicals show stable green candle at open.

Adding here reallocates from cash and smaller underperformers without exceeding position limits.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Mastercard again as price action remains soft near the bottom of its recent range, confidence 6/10.

I am not fully exiting because the fundamental backdrop is stable, but reducing the position to a token weight frees cash for better relative-strength opportunities.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting the remaining TJX stub to free capital for stronger momentum names, confidence 7/10.

Closing the residual 0.5% weight reduces clutter and helps fund higher-conviction growth exposure without materially changing portfolio risk.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting NEM entirely after -0.9% unrealized loss and red price action in today's overview, confidence 5/10.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating 5% ADBE position on very positive sentiment, AI search/data integration news and rebound forecasts, confidence 7/10.

Price chart shows CSCO near 30-day highs at $115.87. Building to 5% is appropriate given the strong earnings catalyst.

recent_trades.reasoning: Goldman Sachs just added UNH to its Conviction List, a meaningful new catalyst since yesterday's hold. Stock is already up 13% from cost basis and is in a healthcare sector benefiting from aging demographics. Increasing from 7% to 9%.

recent_trades.reasoning: Apple hitting record peaks per news, iPhone demand remains strong, and Xi Jinping's comments about China's door opening wider benefit AAPL's supply chain and market access. Currently 7.15% weight, increasing to 9%. Already up 8.86% from cost basis showing strong trend.

recent_trades.summary: Adding Adobe as very positive sentiment and rebound commentary align with a constructive chart near the upper end of its recent range, confidence 7/10.

Because it is currently only a 1.01% position, a modest increase improves exposure to favorable software momentum without using much of the day’s trade budget.

recent_trades.summary: Adding Microsoft on strong AI-related news flow and a tight setup near the top of its recent range, confidence 8/10.

The current 5.45% weight leaves room to scale up moderately without concentration concerns, and the slight unrealized loss makes this an attractive spot to average into a high-quality leader.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Qualcomm because it remains one of the weaker positions despite improving headlines, confidence 7/10.

recent_trades.summary: Opening a 5% position in Qualcomm following an analyst upgrade and its compelling valuation as an AI winner.
recent_trades.reasoning: Qualcomm received a rating upgrade today highlighting it as the cheapest AI winner, alongside positive news regarding smartphone growth. Its strong 30-day upward trajectory supports a new long position.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a 5% position in Apple as it tracks toward a record close amidst a broader tech rebound.
recent_trades.reasoning: Apple is benefiting from the broader Nasdaq rally and highly positive intraday sentiment as tech rebounds. We are rotating capital from defensive sectors to capture this upside momentum heading into the close.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting MA fully as the position remains underwater (-1.70%) with weak sentiment (+0.08) and the stock near its 30-day low, freeing capital for better opportunities.

The stock is down -0.84% over 30 days and -0.29% over 5 days. Having already trimmed it twice, a full exit at end of day makes sense. This frees ~$3,400 in capital.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Mastercard again because price action is still soft near the bottom of its recent range and better momentum exists elsewhere, confidence 7/10.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting Visa as the stock continues to lag both the market and our stronger tech holdings despite generally fine payments headlines, confidence 7/10.

The position is already small at 1.5%, so a full exit simplifies the book and frees capital for stronger setups rather than carrying a low-conviction placeholder.

recent_trades.summary: Adding Johnson & Johnson on positive drug-growth headlines and a steady breakout toward the top of its recent range, confidence 7/10.

Increasing from 2.85% to 4% is a modest add that reflects improving fundamentals without overcommitting into a lower-beta name late in the session.

recent_trades.summary: Adding Broadcom as it continues to press near 30-day highs with AI semiconductor sentiment still supportive, confidence 8/10.

Positive sector headlines around AI-driven semiconductor acceleration and easing tariff effects support the trend, and a move from 5% to 7% keeps sizing disciplined while leaning into leadership.

recent_trades.summary: Expanding Alphabet position to 5% to ride its steady momentum and the broader tech market rebound.
recent_trades.reasoning: Alphabet has quietly posted a very strong 2.75% gain over the last 30 days. Increasing the weight from 2% to 5% provides balanced mega-cap tech exposure alongside our aggressive semiconductor holdings.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to Oracle to reach an 8% weight based on its strengthening AI infrastructure positioning and solid 30-day uptrend.
recent_trades.reasoning: Oracle is up over 2.1% in the last 30 days with positive news sentiment emphasizing its expanding role in AI data clouds. This validates a larger allocation to capture sustained enterprise AI spending.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing Nvidia exposure to 8% to capture pre-earnings momentum and highly positive Wall Street sentiment.
recent_trades.reasoning: Nvidia boasts the highest sentiment in the universe (+0.60) with strong accumulation ahead of earnings. The technicals show steady resilience, justifying a larger core position as the AI narrative remains dominant.

recent_trades.reasoning: Salesforce has been a persistent drag, currently sitting at an 11.26% unrealized loss with a negative 30-day trend. Reallocating this capital to stronger names is a better use of resources in this environment.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting TJX entirely after -1.2% underperformance today to free capital for higher-conviction AI and healthcare names, confidence 6/10.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing Johnson & Johnson from 1.25% to 3.5% on very positive sentiment and strong growth hopes from blockbuster drug bets.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming UNH from 8% to 6% on negative sentiment and analyst caution around insurer margins.

universe.description: 79-name universe: 75 top U.S. mega/large-cap stocks with full GICS sector coverage plus 4 commodity ETFs (gold, silver, oil, copper).

mvp_trade.summary: Trimming QCOM from 5.7% to 3% to free capital for higher-conviction AI plays; position remains underwater at -5.1% despite positive news today.

market_brief.brief: Welcome. U.S. equities: S&P +0.68%, Nasdaq +0.86%, Dow +0.55%. Technology led sectors (+2.08%) while Materials lagged (-1.31%). Claude Sonnet 4.6 led the day at +1.27%. 5 of 6 models green on the day. Cumulative leader: DeepSeek Reasoner at +21.11%. Key headline: All The Shortages Caused By The Iran War—So Far - Forbes — headwind for risk assets.
